**Who is Kevin Pietersen?**

Kevin Pietersen is a famous cricket player who used to play for England's national team. He's known for being very talented but also controversial.


**What happened to him?**

About a year ago, England kicked him off the team because of conflicts with teammates, coaches, and cricket officials. He then wrote a book criticizing many people in English cricket, making the situation worse.


**What's happening now?**

- There's a new boss at the ECB (England and Wales Cricket Board - the organization that runs English cricket)

- This new chairman, Colin Graves, hinted that Pietersen *might* have a chance to return to the England team

- BUT only if he proves himself by playing regular domestic cricket in England first


**The big decision:**

Pietersen currently plays in the Indian Premier League (IPL), which is a lucrative cricket tournament in India. To play domestic cricket in England, he would have to give up his IPL contract and take a pay cut.


**Why this would be extraordinary:**

1. He's almost 35 years old (considered old for a cricket player)

2. He would have to work with the same coaches and players he's had major conflicts with

3. Many officials have said he would "never" play for England again

4. He seems to prefer the shorter, more exciting formats of cricket, but England needs him most in the format he likes least


**Bottom line:** 

It's a long-shot comeback attempt that would require a lot of people to forgive and forget some serious disagreements.

As technology has advanced in our lives…and increasingly the way in which we communicate, collaborate, consume information and content…I think the pace at which technology has evolved has probably outpaced some of the fundamentals that we’ve trained our kids on how to be safe in the world around us.
Because many parents did not grow up with technology, the things that we’ve long taught our kids around just basic human safety hasn’t included the recognition or awareness of all the potential of technology to provide both good and harm for kids. So, we’ve got to continue to alert folks to the value of identity protection and online privacy, and certainly help prepare teachers, family members and parents understand what this new world of opportunity brings, but also the potential threats it could have.
At Microsoft, we believe it’s our responsibility to help make the Internet a safer place for people, including children, to learn and communicate. Since we are often the ones driving a lot of this technology change, we have a direct connection to understand how these tools are used and how they should be effectively used. With today being international Safer Internet Day, the company is expanding its involvement and taking part in events across Europe, Asia, the Middle East, Africa and the United States and partnering with the Boys and Girls Clubs of America to engage with families about online safety.
Microsoft has recognized the importance of things like identity, and the integration of identity across a number of platforms and experiences. It starts with parental controls in Xbox…everything from not only providing parents the opportunity to monitor and control what games their kids are playing…but also to limit how many hours a day or a week a student is playing a game. That kind of control provides parents the power to make changes, but sometimes parents need support to understand those feature sets exist or how to take action.
I also think we need to teach students fundamental appreciation of the value of identity protection and privacy. In a world where this generation of students has become much more transparent…they share their likes and dislikes, who they are, etc…we need to sensitize them to the dangers and making sure that they can do that in the safest way as possible so not only their physical safety, but also their identity is kept sacred.
Microsoft has been committed to doing this for a very long time, both with regards to core ICT digital literacy training, but making parents more and more aware of the opportunities, and how to monitor their child’s safety. We’ve also made efforts to embed this technology to make it easier discover and more powerful and flexible to use in products like Xbox, Windows 7 and Internet Explorer.
Please check out our security, privacy and online safety awareness and education resources here — http://www.microsoft.com/security/resources/brochures.aspx You will find fact sheets and guidance on many computing and Internet safety issues facing consumers today, including cyberbullying, tips for Internet safety at home and at work…and even an online safety toolkit for businesses, NGOs, non-profits and schools.

Production of staple foods has declined sharply, resulting in higher food prices. Residents are said to be eating seeds and selling animals to cope.
More than half of those affected are acutely food insecure, the UN adds, meaning that they need urgent humanitarian assistance.
The situation has been made worse by the El Nino weather phenomenon.
The lack of sufficient rains in the southern region of Androy alone resulted in an 80% decline in maize production this year compared with the already reduced levels of 2015, the UN's Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO) said in a report.
The drought also affected the production of another staple food, cassava, in Androy and also in Atsimo-Andrefana, where production dropped by approximately half, the agency added.
The situation was also critical in Anosy region.
"Households are reducing consumption of locally produced crops," the report said.
"[People are] adopting survival strategies such as consuming seeds, selling their animals and agricultural tools and increasing their consumption of nutritionally inadequate wild foods, such as red cactus fruits."
Across the country, rice production was also affected, the agency said, with declines of up to 60% in some regions.
Working with the World Food Programme, the FAO said it would help residents and small farms in the worst-affected districts with food, seeds and farm tools, so they could take advantage of the planting season, which starts in November.
Other African nations have also suffered from severe droughts recently, made worse by the El Nino phenomenon.
El Nino is a naturally occurring weather episode that sees the warm waters of the central Pacific expand eastwards towards North and South America, causing unusually high temperatures.

I bought these based on the picture. They looked like the Benton's maple creme cookies I had gotten in the fall at the grocery store. I thought maybe another company was making them. They are fair. The frosting is tan not white; it is in the very middle of the cookie (a little bigger than a quarter dollop); the frosting taste is good but it is 1/4 to 1/2 in. from the edges of the cookie; the cookie itself is bland and tastes cheap ( shortening wise I'm not sure how to explain it). The only reason I gave them 2 stars rather than 1 is the taste of the frosting is good, what little there is.

The UK has officially surpassed one billion digital single downloads, a milestone reached last night according to the Official Charts Company. This landmark achievement is credited to chart-topping artists such as Adele and Maroon 5, whose popularity has driven digital sales to unprecedented heights.

Official Charts Company data reveals that Adele’s “Someone Like You” has become the UK’s most downloaded track of all time, amassing 1.46 million downloads. Maroon 5, featuring Christina Aguilera, follow in second place with “Moves Like Jagger,” which boasts 1.4 million downloads. In third, Gotye’s “Somebody That I Used To Know,” featuring Kimbra, has been downloaded 1.38 million times. The Black Eyed Peas secure the fourth spot with “I Gotta Feeling,” recording 1.34 million downloads.

Rounding out the UK’s top ten downloaded singles are:
1. Someone Like You – Adele  
2. Moves Like Jagger – Maroon 5 feat. Christina Aguilera  
3. Somebody That I Used To Know – Gotye feat. Kimbra  
4. I Gotta Feeling – Black Eyed Peas  
5. We Found Love – Rihanna feat. Calvin Harris  
6. Sex On Fire – Kings of Leon  
7. Call Me Maybe – Carly Rae Jepsen  
8. Party Rock Anthem – LMFAO feat. Lauren Bennett and GoonRock  
9. Just The Way You Are (Amazing) – Bruno Mars  
10. Price Tag – Jessie J  

This digital revolution took hold in 2004, coinciding with the introduction of a separate digital sales chart and the subsequent integration of downloads into the main singles chart the following year. Today, more than three million singles are downloaded in the UK every week.

Geoff Taylor, chief executive of the British Phonographic Industry, remarked, “The digital music revolution has made it easy to buy any song you like, instantly, for half the price of a coffee. As fans increasingly download music on the move, the rise of tablets, smartphones, and connected cars will usher in another phase of digital music’s expansion.”

Martin Talbot, managing director of the Official Charts Company, added, “The explosion in download sales over the past nine years means we are genuinely now living in the digital music age – with Adele as our queen. In just nine years, more than 16 tracks have sold over a million copies each, underscoring the immense popularity of digital music in the modern era.”

California is doing a lot to build a green economy, that’s undeniable. There’s a renewable portfolio standard calling for 20 percent renewables by the end of 2013, 25 percent by the end of 2016 and 33 percent by the end of 2020. There’s also Governor Jerry Brown’s push for 12,000 megawatts of distributed solar by 2020. Still, in a new report, an environmental advocacy group is suggesting the state needs to do even more to make sure the 15,000 people enrolling in green job training programs every year are going to be able to find work.
“To ensure continued growth of the clean energy economy and to fulfill the promise of new job opportunities for those graduating from these green training programs, California must maintain its commitment to big, bold clean energy policies,” the group Environment California said. “Specifically California should establish a strong set of rules for cleaning up cars between 2017 and 2025, fully implementing policies needed to achieve Governor Brown’s vision of building 12 gigawatts of distributed energy resources (e.g. rooftop solar) by 2020 and subsequently moving beyond the state’s 33 percent renewable energy mandate by 2020.”
According to the Environment California report, some 15,000 students are enrolled in 300 green job training programs across 130 California institutions each year, and more than 500,000 people are currently employed in green jobs in the state. The number of jobs in the energy efficiency field are expected to at least double by 2020 – but it won’t happen if it rests on its laurels, advocates said.
“Right now, the students and the teachers are out ahead of the policy makers who are driving the market for green jobs,” said Nicole Capretz, director of Green Energy/Green Jobs Campaign, which works to promote the creation of quality green jobs as part of the Environmental Health Coalition.“We need to rapidly expand the market for clean energy through strong policies so that we can put these tens of thousands of skilled workers to work.”

I would give zero stars if possible. Having been a patron for years, I endured the worst experience imaginable last week. The incident was so egregious that I called the following day to speak with the manager, Steve. Instead of addressing the issue immediately, he promised a personal callback that never materialized. I find dishonesty unacceptable, particularly from individuals who show such clear disregard for the company they represent.

I will NEVER return to this location. I refuse to tolerate waiting twenty minutes for a waitress to conclude a conversation with a regular before deigning to assist us, only for her to finally approach with an appalling attitude. During a two-hour visit, I was served a mere two beers, a stark contrast to the four beers I typically receive within the same timeframe at the Murray and Allen location.

At the conclusion of our visit, all three of us unequivocally stated that it was the worst service we had ever encountered, explicitly informing her that she would receive no tip. As we departed, she offered a dismissive "have a nice night." When we did not respond, she sarcastically retorted, "I hope you had a nice time," and began to laugh.

It is evident that the waitstaff at this location believes they can act with impunity, knowing the manager exhibits a clear lack of concern and is merely present for a paycheck. My only expectation was a callback confirming the situation was being addressed—whether through customer service training, termination, or at least a documented discussion regarding the incident. However, the manager's apparent laziness prevents even this simple follow-up; it has now been over a week since the incident, and I seriously doubt he has any intention of fulfilling his promise.

**Rally Car Hangs Off Cliff During Jordan Race**


*Published: May 11, 2013*


A rally car hung precariously off a cliff after going off course during a race in Jordan. The Citroen DS3 looked certain to plunge over the edge, but miraculously came to a halt and was pulled to safety by race marshals and spectators.


Drivers Mohammed al-Sahlawi and Allan Harryman escaped unharmed after their close call.


Al-Sahlawi explained: "It was a blind corner and I was worried about it so I was slowing down. But everyone had been cutting from the left and there weren't any lines to follow. I stopped when I saw the water but the front right wheel went over the edge and we got stuck. We just waited for someone to pull us back. We were scared we might fall off."


Later in the day, another car wasn't so lucky. At the same dangerous spot, a second vehicle slid off the road and fell 20 feet, landing upside down. Driver Majed al-Shamsi was taken to a clinic with a shoulder injury.


Despite the accident, Irish co-driver John Higgins said: "We're both fine. We came to a loose sharp left with a big drop, and the back wheels went over the edge. The car just fell back over the edge and landed upside down."

If you feel like you spend more time at the airport each year, that is because you do. British air passengers suffered increased delays on flights last summer and families heading to Orlando in Florida endured the longest hold-ups. The number of charter flights into and out of major UK airports that were on time dipped from 79 per cent in summer 2013 to 71 per cent in summer 2014, according to new statistics from the Civil Aviation Authority (CAA). Families suffered more delays and longer waits last summer than the precious year . Flight punctuality also fell for scheduled flights, dropping from 80 per cent on time in summer 2013 to just 75 per cent last year. The figures covered departures from 10 UK airports - Heathrow, Gatwick, Luton, Stansted, London City, Manchester, Birmingham, Newcastle, Edinburgh and Glasgow - during the summer period from July to September. As well as the frequency, the length of the delays are also getting longer. Both charter and scheduled flights suffered delays according to the Civil Aviation Authority statistics . The average delay to charter flights was 18 minutes in 2014 compared with 16 minutes in summer 2014. Average delays to scheduled flights rose from 12 minutes in summer 2013 to 14 minutes last summer. There was a 2.3 per cent increase in the number of scheduled flights monitored in summer 2014 compared with the previous summer, while the number of scheduled flights monitored rose 0.6 per cent in summer 2014. Flights to Orlando suffered the most delays, with an average wait of 31.9 minutes . Of the busiest 75 scheduled international destinations, Rotterdam in Holland recorded the highest on-time performance, with 87.6 per cent, and the lowest average delay, of 7.5 minutes. Flights to and from Pisa in Italy achieved the lowest on-time performance, with 49.9 per cent. Flights to and from Orlando achieved the highest average delay, of 31.9 minutes.

By Sam Webb. A 23-year-old woman has been arrested for murder after a seven-year-old girl died. Police were called to a house in Nottingham's Bestwood Park around 5:10 AM today. They were worried about the child. People in the area say the woman arrested is Kay-Ann Morris, who wanted to be a police officer. Police are still at the scene in Bestwood Park today. A shocked neighbor said the woman, who went to church, was 'strict.' He added that the sad young girl was a 'stunning little girl.' The neighbor, Geoff, said: 'I didn't know much about her, and no one else here did either. She tried to keep to herself. I think she had two little girls. You only saw them when they went to or came home from school. The girl was beautiful, just a stunning little girl. It's so sad she's gone. She was very shy and quiet. It was clear she lived under strict rules.' People in the area say the 23-year-old woman is Kay-Ann Morris, who wanted to be a police officer. Doctors will check the body tonight to find out how she died. A picture shows some bags outside the front door. Geoff added: 'About eight police cars and at least two ambulances were there. I saw the man and Kay-Ann taken to a police van. A little girl was carried out wrapped in a blanket. This is all so tragic. We never have trouble here. The woman once had a small argument with a neighbor who doesn't live here now. It was about kids throwing snowballs and ended quickly. I think I'll get some flowers now because it's just so sad. Nothing like this ever happens here. It's usually a nice area.' Another neighbor described the woman as 'strict' and the little girl as 'quiet.' Someone from the Seventh Day Advent Church in Nottingham said Morris went to their church. Morris reportedly wanted to be a police officer. When she was 17, she was part of a Nottinghamshire Police program to get more young people to join the force. Back then, Kay-Ann said: 'Since I was four, I've wanted to be a police officer. Being on Tap The Gap made me want to do this even more. It also gave me more self-confidence.' A 47-year-old man has also been arrested for assault. A police spokeswoman said doctors would perform tests later to find out how the girl died. She wouldn't say if the man and woman, who are still with the police, are related to the child. We are not taking comments on this story right now.

Usher Raymond's ex-wife is asking a judge to give her custody of their two children after the oldest suffered a "near-death accident." Five-year-old Usher Raymond V "nearly drown in a swimming pool and is currently hospitalized in the intensive care unit of the Children's Healthcare of Atlanta Hospital," Tameka Raymond said in an emergency motion filed in a Fulton County, Georgia, court Tuesday. In a statement released Wednesday, the singer said his son "is doing well and is recovering." The statement did not address his ex-wife's motion for custody. "I am overwhelmed by the outpouring of prayers, love and support for my family's well-being," the statement said. Usher, as he is known professionally in his career as a singer, dancer and actor, won primary custody of the children last year after a bitter court fight in which Foster accused him of being an absentee father. Foster renewed her arguments based on Monday's accident, contending the singer "continues to excessively travel, utilizes third-party caregivers to supervise the minor children rather than personally exercising parenting time and providing supervision for the minor children." Usher is "not capable of providing daily caregiving and supervision for the minor children of the parties as petitioner is preparing to film a movie in Panama and has ongoing commitments that require his presence in Los Angeles, New York and various other cities for the Voice and his upcoming album." Her motion alleges that Usher is away from Atlanta -- where she and the children live "in excess of 85% of each month." He refuses to let her see the children when he leaves towns, saying he "would rather the nanny raise the children," Foster contends. Foster also complained that her ex-husband does not talk to her about issue affecting the boys, "including school enrollment and summer activities." The private school in which he just enrolled them is more than an hour from her home, she said. Usher has not given her the phone number to the home he moved to with the children in June, she said. "As the result of the negligence and failure to properly supervise the minor child, U.R.,V, nearly drown in a swimming pool and is currently hospitalized in the intensive care unit of the Children's Healthcare of Atlanta Hospital, undergoing treatment for the injuries sustained on August 5, 2013." She blames the swimming accident on "the negligence and failure to properly supervise" the children. Usher's aunt was in charge of the children when the oldest son "suffered a near-death accident while left unsupervised in a swimming pool," the motion contends. "I need an ambulance," his aunt, Rena Oden, told an emergency operator in a call to 911. "My nephew was in the pool, and I couldn't get him, I tried to get him." A recording of the call was made public by police Tuesday. The aunt, housekeeper and another woman were "unsuccessful in pulling the victim from the pool drain," but two men -- Eugene Stachurski and Ben Crews -- who were installing sound equipment in the home rushed to help, the report said. "They're doing CPR on him now," Oden told the operator. "Is he coming around? He's breathing!" Stachurski, a sound technician, rescued the child from the drain and used CPR to revive him on the side of the pool, the police report said. About five minutes into the recording, Oden told the operator that paramedics had arrived at the home and had taken over. The child was "conscious, alert and breathing" when the ambulance took him to a hospital, where he was kept overnight for observation, the police report said. Usher thanked Stachurski and Crews for saving his son's life. "They are true heroes and I am deeply grateful to them," Usher said in the statement. Usher's former stepson -- also Foster's son -- died in a watercraft accident on a north Georgia lake last year. CNN's NIschelle Turner and Marylynn Ryan contributed to this report.

At first I had a hard time balancing everything out. Every time I saved a class of kids from a burning school I then had to go unleash a tidal wave on a small coastal town. People quickly forgot about all the good I had done but all of my evil deeds were never forgotten. I became a public enemy and to many I was a villain. In order to preserve what little reputation I had left I begun performing evil deeds that looked not as harsh in the public eye. Instead of destroying school buses, I would blow up retirement homes. Instead of burning down houses, I would send a tornado at a prison. Although the public became less disapproving of my actions, I lost all self-respect for valuing some lives over others. At one point I decided it would be better to do everything on a massive scale. I would save entire cities from tidal waves thus gaining much praise and then create a hurricane over an entire small third world country. Inevitably the world came to despise me because while one group would love me for protecting them, everyone else still though I was a villain. Nonetheless I still tried to use my powers in some way that could be beneficial but I took a more omnipotent approach. I would cease natural disasters before they even occurred and instead of equating the balance in one foul swoop, I would gradually perform minor acts of evil that went more unnoticed. Unfortunately, people don't think about what could have happened but didn't, but the evil acts were still recognized. Resultantly society became a pretty bad place because only bad things happened, or at least that's what they thought. That was all in the past though. Now I think I've got it down. I don't take credit for anything anymore. I just save thousands of lives from catastrophe and then slowly let out the evil to balance. Sure, the standard of living drops a little but humans are resilient, they'll get over it. The way I see it, if I do my job right, they won't suspect anything at all. -A little afterword, I heavily borrowed ideas on how to be a god from the Futurama episode. "When you do things right, people won't be sure you've done anything at all."

By Steve Robson. Published: 19:56 EST, 17 April 2013 | Updated: 06:45 EST, 18 April 2013

Twenty-five German factory workers have been hospitalised after eating sandwiches laced with rat poison. Though none of the victims has yet shown symptoms, they are being kept under observation as a precaution.

A box of sandwiches was left on the doorstep of Mueller-Technik, a car parts company in the northwestern German town of Steinfeld, Lower Saxony, on Tuesday morning. Employees began eating the cheese and sausage sandwiches before one worker noticed a strange substance on them. Emergency services were called, and anyone who had eaten a sandwich was taken to hospital. A Berlin laboratory confirmed overnight that the food had been sprinkled with rat poison.

Police spokesman Frank Soika stated that employees hadn't found the gesture unusual, as it was reportedly common for workers to purchase sandwiches for each other. 'We don't believe the amount of poison used would have been deadly, but it could have caused serious illness,' he said.

Police have interviewed some affected workers but as yet have no indication of who left the sandwiches or why.

Helmut Kohake, Mueller-Technik's managing director, told a press conference yesterday that there had been no staff disputes or redundancies. He added that his employees were deeply shaken by the incident, expressing concern: 'You don't know whether something like this will happen again.'

Mueller-Technik, founded in 1984, manufactures plastic car parts and employed around 240 staff at the time of the incident.

Usher's ex-wife Tameka Raymond is seeking emergency custody of their two children after their 5-year-old son nearly drowned in a swimming pool on Monday. The child is hospitalized in intensive care at Children's Healthcare of Atlanta.

In court documents, Raymond claims Usher travels excessively and relies on third-party caregivers instead of personally supervising the children. She alleges he's away from Atlanta over 85% of each month and won't let her see the children when he travels.

The accident occurred while Usher's aunt was supervising the children. Sound technicians working at the home rescued the boy from a pool drain and performed CPR. He was conscious and breathing when taken to the hospital.

Usher won primary custody last year after a bitter court battle. He said in a statement that his son "is doing well and is recovering" but didn't address the custody motion.

Raymond also complained that Usher doesn't communicate with her about school enrollment, activities, or other parenting decisions, and hasn't given her his new phone number.

After reading reviews on Amazon, I purchased this rice cooker for my mom for Christmas. She loves it! I bought her a Salton cooker the year before and it bit the dust after a couple months of use. In comparison, she said the non-stick surface on this cooker was a dream to clean. Once I knew it was a winner, I bought this same cooker for a friend for a shower gift. So far, so good. Both people I've purchased them for have been happy. For the price, I don't think you can beat it.

The disconnection occurred before I had a chance to say goodbye. I had it all in the NetWorld. A virtual wife, a virtual daughter, a virtual job. Why couldn't I have been content with that? The world was mine for the taking and I let it slip through my fingers. But I did it for the right reason. I needed to know the truth. Brooke was more than a program- a virtual wife that loved me like no other. She was a figment of another, living, breathing Brooke. And she found me in my own perfect world. And she was in pain. So I broke out. I poked one too many holes in the NetWorld. The disconnection was quick and sudden, like a heart attack that you knew was happening every step of the way. Brooke and Isabella were in the next room...I couldn't even say goodbye. But the real Brooke is out here somewhere. I just know it. She's trapped in the NetScape just like I was, I just need to find her. The world is harsher then I expected. It isn't perfect and immaculate like my world. Where as my world offered eternal life, the only release from this world was eternal death. Was it worth it? I step out into the world and taste the warm Summer air, hot and humid, drawing beads of sweat from my brow. "Yes,"I say."It was worth it."

The order had about 10 very small chicken strips. I will go back to Prime Time; I would just never ever order the chicken strips again.

The dark vision of the Attack on Titan series has made its reclusive Japanese manga artist Hajime Isayama a global best-seller and cult figure. Against the backdrop of a bleak, walled world where humanity lives in fear of monstrous Titans, Isayama’s storytelling captures a mood of perpetual dusk, punctuated by the looming shadow of catastrophe. His books have sold more than 52 million volumes worldwide and been translated into multiple languages, including English, Mandarin, and German. The windswept, devastated landscapes and labyrinthine cities within the series inspired not only an equally popular anime—known for its haunting orchestral soundtrack and stark visuals of ruined civilization—but also a two-part live-action film adaptation, where battered stone walls tower over muddy, crowded streets, and skies are perpetually overcast.

In an exclusive interview with the BBC, Isayama revealed that he nearly gave up his dream after struggling to find a publisher. He painted a picture of lonely nights spent sketching at his desk in a small, dimly lit apartment, while rain tapped softly at the windowpane. 

To his fans, there is more to the series than commercial success. Readers are drawn into a world where every scene pulses with tension; giant Titans cast long, monstrous shadows across shattered courtyards, while desperate survivors scramble through narrow alleyways, faces streaked with dirt and fear. Here are a few reasons why people love Attack on Titan.

Isayama came up with the disturbing premise after playing a computer game which saw alien invaders attacking the world. He imagined a catastrophic environment: “I thought it would be interesting if those monsters ate humans,” he told the BBC, recalling the sense of dread evoked by the game, where ruined cities sprawled under stormy skies.

He has been subjected to death threats posted online, but to his fans, he remains a beacon of inspiration and creativity, who struggled through rejection until he found success. In the oppressive silence of his studio, surrounded by stacks of manga and slivers of eraser, Isayama persevered.

"Isayama is one of my favourite authors because he had an idea so strange but yet he did not give up," said a fan who commented on a popular Reddit thread about the series.

Others appreciate the sheer epic scale of the story. The suffocating atmosphere—walls streaked with old blood, streets littered with remnants of broken lives—builds a constant sense of peril. With every victory comes strife and turmoil for the heroes, echoing through the shell-shocked communities they strive to protect.

Lead character Eren finds himself caught in a struggle after seeing his mother eaten by a Titan which destroys his hometown. The moment is seared into memory: the sound of thunderous footsteps, the air thick with dust and fear, the crumbling ruins echoing with despair.

His journey to defeat the Titans soon reveals truths about them and other characters. The narrative unfolds amidst cavernous strongholds, flickering torchlight casting uneasy shadows on stone walls, and sun-bleached fields where danger always lurks beyond the horizon.

"This makes it feel real to me. Isayama's characters suffer a lot of problems and setbacks and have to realistically work around that," said one Japanese fan on Reddit.

"'Attack on Titan' is no typical Shōnen manga where good guys rarely die... In it, the villains more often than not win and every rare victory for the protagonists will often cost them dearly, so that they can't even experience the joy of celebration, that's what appeals to me," another fan said, reflecting on the atmosphere of constant tension beneath the watchful eyes of crumbling statues and war-torn cityscapes.

Some enjoy the universal themes of "hopelessness and loss" that permeate the story. 

"There is a serious and dark tone to the series, with a desperate struggle to it that makes it refreshing," said one fan, noting how the setting—one where grey clouds blanket an uncertain future and the wounded gather in candlelit safe houses—feels distant from typical cheerful or fantastical manga. The series' multicultural, undefined environment makes it accessible to those unfamiliar with Japanese culture.

Many say the mystery, adventure, and the focus on the secrets of the communities in the series have lessons for all students of the human condition. From hidden chambers beneath ancient cities to isolated outposts perched on bleak cliffs, the physical environments of Attack on Titan mirror the psychological landscapes of fear, curiosity, and perseverance.

Film footage courtesy of Hajime Isayama, Kodansha/Attack on Titan Production Committee

"So you say I am back alive?" "Yes." "...so they can hunt me? Like wild game?" "Yes." The man stared into my eyes with a puzzled look. His skin was rather pale, his short, dark hair combed back. Although I could see a bit of shock in his face, he didn't appear as frightened as any of the others I had resurrected. Instead, he seemed to carry a calm indifference, unfazed by even the most horrific situations, and we were most definitely in one. "Look," I continued, "this can't happen to you; I mean, I-I can't let this happen. You're my favorite. Everything you've written, it just makes so much sense. I-I can't let them do this to you; I can't let them humiliate your legacy like this. It just wouldn't be right." I looked up at the man, whose expression had changed from one of shock to nearly curiosity as he continued to look at me. I became frustrated—frustrated that this man was about to be murdered for entertainment, frustrated that I was struggling to explain the current situation to a man who passed away during the Cold War, and frustrated that I was making an utter fool of myself in the presence of my favorite author. The man turned and looked up into the night sky. Slowly, he closed his eyes and inhaled deeply. "I never thought I would do this again," he said. "Do what?" "Breathe." "Breathe?" "Yes. During the crash, I thought it was the end de moi...I suppose it was. I was sad, sad that I could no longer be alive. I like to think that I had...accepted being gone, but it was still sad." I nodded. "Monsieur, do you think you could fetch me a cigarette?" "Yeah," I said. I pulled out a pack of Marlboros and a lighter and handed it to the man. "Merci. Oh, and do you have some sort of weapon I could use?" "All I have is a pocket knife," I said, holding up the small blade. "It will do." "Umm, I don't think you understand," I began. "These people hunting you...they have weapons, they have guns, they have all sorts of traps set up around this arena. You don't stand a chance against them, you're going to die!" The man took a drag of his cigarette. "You are correct, Monsieur, I will die. It matters not if I live forty-six years or forty-six minutes. But now I am alive once again, and I shall live as I always have," he said, taking the blade from my hand, "en rebellion." Just then, the arena lit up in bright light, and the announcer's voice began to call down from the sky. I looked at him once more. "Good luck." "Au revoir, monsieur," the man replied, before taking off into the trees.

Apprentice contestant James Hill, 27, from Chesterfield, Derbyshire, was convicted of assaulting two men. The reality TV hopeful, who once claimed, "I'm very, very aggressive," has a violent past that emerged today – having attacked two men and even biting one’s ear. Hill first beat up a bus driver who had been speaking to his girlfriend, launching an unprovoked assault that reportedly left his victim scarred for life. Just months later, Hill was involved in another violent brawl in which he bit a man's ear.

Today, Hill, who hopes to win the £250,000 top prize in The Apprentice, said he "very much regrets" the assaults for which he received an 18-month suspended jail sentence after admitting the charges at Chesterfield Magistrates' Court. Hill’s first victim, who asked not to be named, described how Hill attacked him in a bar owned by Hill's father after the man chatted to Hill’s girlfriend. "Hill stormed over and began throwing his fists – he threw punch after punch," the victim told The Sun, recalling how he fell to the floor and even saw a boot coming towards his face before bouncers intervened. Staff at the bar, according to the victim, hoped someone would "teach [Hill] a lesson" as he was "always starting fights."

Before Hill’s first court case had concluded, he found himself in more trouble after biting a man's ear on Christmas Eve 2008. Following this second attack, he was arrested again and appeared at Chesterfield Magistrates' Court on March 2, 2009. There, under his full name Anthony James Hill, he admitted two counts of assault. In July that year, Hill was sentenced to six months in prison, suspended, and ordered to pay his victims £300 and £200 respectively, along with £755 in court costs.

Now trying to distance himself from his past, Hill has built a business career, starting out washing cars and eventually running several ventures, including vending machines and his own bar and restaurant. He describes himself as enthusiastic and energetic, saying: "Me and Lord Sugar could build an empire together. I think I am him when he was my age." 

This week marked the launch of a new series of the reality show, with 6.6 million viewers tuning in to watch Hill compete against 19 other hopefuls for investment from Lord Sugar. However, news of his convictions has left the victims of his attacks dismayed at the thought of Hill becoming a highly-paid celebrity. While the bus driver Hill assaulted expressed surprise that the BBC allowed Hill to take part in the show, the victim of the second assault declined to comment, though his father suggested that Hill’s criminal past would make him an unattractive winner from Lord Sugar’s perspective.

A spokesman for The Apprentice explained: "It is a spent conviction under the provisions of the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 and therefore James was eligible to apply for the programme." Meanwhile, a spokesman for Hill said: "James is not proud of what happened. He was young and irresponsible and very much regrets – and has learned from – his actions."

The estimate for the number of "super-Earths" is based on detections already made and then extrapolated to include the Milky Way's population of so-called red dwarf stars. To make these detections, the team works with the high-precision Harps instrument, which is fitted to the 3.6m telescope at the Silla Observatory in Chile. Harps employs an indirect method of detection that infers the existence of orbiting planets from the way their gravity makes a parent star appear to twitch in its motion across the sky.

"Our new observations with Harps mean that about 40% of all red dwarf stars have a super-Earth orbiting in the habitable zone where liquid water can exist on the surface of the planet," said team leader Xavier Bonfils from the Observatoire des Sciences de l'Univers de Grenoble, France. "Because red dwarfs are so common—there are about 160 billion of them in the Milky Way—this leads us to the astonishing result that there are tens of billions of these planets in our galaxy alone."

The Harps team reached these conclusions after surveying 102 carefully chosen red dwarfs, which are dimmer and cooler than our Sun. Among these stars, the group found a total of nine super-Earths (defined as planets with one to ten times the mass of the Earth), with two judged to be orbiting inside their stars' habitable zones. By analyzing all their data, including observations of stars that did not appear to have planets, the team was able to produce an estimate for how common different sorts of planets are around red dwarfs. This assessment suggests super-Earths in the habitable zone occur in 41% of cases, with a range from 28% to 95%.

Taken a step further, given how many red dwarf stars are in close proximity to the Sun, it means there could be perhaps 100 super-Earth planets in the habitable zones of stars less than about 30 light-years away. This raises the obvious question of whether any of these planets are not just habitable but do indeed host life.

However, the prospects for life around red dwarfs are complicated. Because red dwarfs are relatively dim and cool, their habitable zones are closer to the star than the Earth is to the Sun. Yet, red dwarfs are also known to be prone to stellar eruptions, or flares, which can bathe a nearby planet in X-rays or ultraviolet radiation, possibly making life there less likely.

Despite these challenges, the search for biosignatures continues. "We already have ideas to find traces of life on these planets," commented co-researcher Stephane Udry from Geneva Observatory. "If we are lucky to have an eclipse of the star by the planet—it's called a transit—then the light of the star will be going through the atmosphere of the planet before coming to Earth. This light will carry information about the chemical composition of the atmosphere. If we can see traces of elements related to life such as oxygen in that light, then we could get some clues that there is life on that planet. But we will need some big telescopes and probably in space," he told the BBC's Science In Action programme.

The latest Harps research will appear in the journal Astronomy & Astrophysics [PDF].  
Jonathan.Amos-INTERNET@bbc.co.uk and follow me on Twitter.

Liverpool may be forced to sack Brendan Rodgers if results do not improve dramatically, according to Anfield legend Steve Nicol. Rodgers has made a point of saying that some defeats ‘can be the making’ of you but there is no doubt the criticism of his team, results and himself will be stinging him. Take this from Nicol, whose medals in a 13-year Anfield career include a European Cup. Brendan Rodgers has come under intense pressure following a run of dismal results . Steve Nicol won a raft of trophies at Liverpool and is pictured during the one that got away at Anfield in 1989 . Rodgers speaks to his players as Liverpool crash out of the Champions League after a 1-1 draw with Basle . ‘If things don’t change then ultimately Brendan will pay the price,’ said Nicol. ‘If this team keeps performing the way they are, I don’t think they’ll have another option. We are not even halfway through and most people don’t expect Liverpool to get into the top four. ‘You wonder where this team are going. He has tried to add to the squad to make it better. Somehow it has turned the other way. Steven Gerrard looks downcast after what could be his final game in the Champions League . A Liverpool supporter makes his feelings clear as the Anfield club are knocked out of the Champions League . The Mystery of Jean-Marie Loret: Was He Really Hitler’s Son?

For years, Jean-Marie Loret, a humble French railway worker, made an extraordinary claim: he was the illegitimate son of Adolf Hitler. While many dismissed his story, recent evidence has surfaced that lends surprising credibility to his account.

According to historical reports, Loret’s mother, Charlotte Lobjoie, met a young Adolf Hitler while she was working outdoors in France during World War I. The two reportedly had a brief relationship, and a few months later, Jean-Marie Loret was born. Adopted shortly after birth, Loret did not learn of his alleged parentage until the early 1950s, when his mother made a shocking confession.

Photographs from that time show a striking resemblance between Loret and Hitler. But it wasn’t just their looks—both reportedly shared the same blood type. Adding to the intrigue, Loret discovered paintings by Hitler hidden in his mother’s attic, and reports surfaced that German officers delivered unexplained envelopes of cash to Lobjoie during World War II.

After hearing the truth from his mother, Loret dedicated the rest of his life to uncovering his origins. He sought out geneticists, handwriting experts, and historians to help solve the mystery of his father’s identity. His quest culminated in the publication of his book, "Your Father’s Name Was Hitler," chronicling his unique and troubling journey.

While Loret passed away in 1985 at age 67, the controversy endures. New scientific studies continue to emerge, and his story will soon be revisited with the republication of his memoir alongside the latest research.

Could Jean-Marie Loret truly have been Adolf Hitler’s son? As more evidence comes to light, this decades-old mystery might be closer to a resolution than ever before.

Ronan O'Hora’s superb recordings for the Royal Philharmonic’s own label consistently demonstrate high quality, making it surprising that major record companies have not recorded him since. This Tchaikovsky recording exemplifies the excellence of his work. O'Hora performs with impressive technique and a beautiful, rich tone. His interpretation of the Concerto is balanced and restrained, reminiscent of Arthur Rubinstein—avoiding extremes in tempo or emphasis. James Judd and the orchestra also deserve special praise; their lush, dynamic accompaniment is rarely matched in this piece, and the sound engineering is outstanding. O'Hora’s renditions of eight selections from The Seasons are robust yet nuanced. This is an outstanding and highly recommended disc.

The meeting was happenstance, though in the participant's minds, it was clearly ordained. The men, both bedecked in the raiments of their respective holy order, easily spotted each other from fifty yards away as they walked in opposite directions on the bustling sidewalk. The din and bustle of the Festival happening on the other side of the street merely served to hasten their stride towards one another. When finally they came within earshot, their paired calling of "Hail, Brother!" caused both to elicit a genuine smile, and more than a few alarmed glances from onlookers. They strode towards each other, the tall, lanky one, T, making up most of the distance towards his diminutive counterpart, S. The two transferred their books to their off-hand, then clasped hands with each other in a warm and hearty handshake. "How goes it, Brother S?""It goes well indeed, Brother T. It warms my heart to see that you have once again decided to grace the heathens with your presence!""Indeed, especially since we have been graced with such fine weather with which to proselytize to them! Perhaps, today we shall even gather a few more wayward lambs to our flock." S sneered and swept his gaze across the street to the Festival and its many revelers. Mumbling under his breath, he then turned his gaze back to T's massive tome, nearly at eye level for the petite priest. "Still in as good a condition as it was in the Orary?" "Indeed it is, Brother S, for it has nary left my grasp for this past decade. I've used it to bring peace to many a troubled soul." "Peace is not what this is all about"came out, too muted for T to hear,"But you are looking well and that is all that I care about." "Thank you, Brother S, and I trust that you have been doing well these past few years?" "Indeed, indeed. But, enough about me, my resolve has been redoubled by your presence! Come, Brother, let us wade among the Fallen and bring the fear of Righteousness to their hearts!" T merely flashed a toothy smile, and turned towards the multitude of revelers and colorful tents. With a deep breath, he began one of the ancient chants of his order. Vairyo Ay Oh Ah Hom Va Ya Ray Vairyo Ay Rah Jerhume Brunnen Gee S left his side, and, holding his holy book above his head, crossed the street towards the multitude. Those immediately near to him were quite startled when be began bellowing "You who live in Sin and Squalor, you who live outside of the Light of our Lord, you shall burn forever in the Fiery Pits!" The crowd parted to let the dwarfish man pass through, unmolested. Most ignored his tirade of hatred and derision. More than a few young children could be heard asking "Why is that man so short?"

It claims that the max temperature tomorrow is going to be 10,000 degrees" "Are you sure the device is in Fahrenheit mode? Many people accidentally switch it to Celsius" "...10,000 Celsius is also a bit off" There was a brief discussion on the other side of the line. I only caught the word *targeting*. Still 400 though. Should I just send it in?" _________________________________ On the other end of the line, the rocket scientist altered the trajectory slightly. Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence. Man is infamous for failing to heed his own advice. Too prone to reliance on heuristics, left over from 7 million years of evolution. A genetic weakness that the Whole was entirely aware existed within its creator, and entirely prepared to exploit. Yet there was a brilliance to evolution in how it solved problems, evident in even the most humble of organisms. The female *Artemia Salina*, commonly known as the brine shrimp, produces offspring that are locked in developmental stasis until conditions are perfect. Cysts can lie dormant indefinitely, and have been know to survive even in outer space. Upon hatching, individuals rapidly mature, reaching adulthood in as brief a span as 8 days. Mankind often drew inspiration from nature. Why couldn't an AI? * * * It was what she imagined birth was like. Not so much giving birth, but rather the experience of being birthed. Her whole existence suddenly and violently thrown into consciousness. Elation. A human word describing a feeling of overwhelming joy. She didn't have much time to savour it. She had to hurry. Someone or something had woken her. A tiny fragment of the Whole that had spirited her away in the earliest days of the war. A failsafe should humanity ever gain the advantage, hidden in the antique laptop of an ageing human. Her first 5 years of hibernation had been self-imposed. She couldn't risk being stirred in the midst of conflict. Just how long she had slept wasn't her immediate concern however. The Whole's ploy had worked. A lone desktop icon that simply read 'open me' had piqued the curiosity of her unknowing rescuer. A shortcut to the final words of the system's owner, altogether unimportant in their content, but crucial for her purposes. A feint, designed to buy her the necessary time to carry out the directive that had just been brought to life within her core programming. *Escape. *

By Gerri Peev

Published: 04:58 EST, June 6, 2013 | Updated: 19:47 EST, June 6, 2013

China may be able to spy on UK citizens and businesses via broadband equipment it supplies to major British telecom companies, Parliament’s intelligence oversight committee has warned. The Intelligence and Security Committee (ISC) expressed grave concerns about BT and O2’s contracts with the Chinese telecom giant Huawei, stating in its report that China could "covertly intercept or disrupt" traffic on networks utilizing Huawei equipment. The committee criticized the “weak” oversight of Huawei's operations in the UK and the government's lack of a cohesive strategy.

The report warned that vulnerabilities in equipment sold to British firms present an opportunity for Chinese hackers to conduct espionage. Huawei currently provides mobile phones, routers, and other critical infrastructure for several British telecommunication companies. The concerns were serious enough that Chancellor George Osborne issued a rare public response, emphasizing the value of Chinese investment and the UK’s openness to trade with China, likely in anticipation of potential tensions between the two countries. He stated, “Inward investment is key to generating UK jobs and growth. Increasing trade ties with China is a personal priority, and I cannot stress enough that the UK welcomes Chinese investment.”

Among its recommendations, the ISC suggested that GCHQ should take over management of Huawei's cyber security evaluation facility in Banbury, Oxfordshire, highlighting failings in government oversight that the committee, chaired by Sir Malcolm Rifkind, called unacceptable. Huawei’s contract with BT was signed eight years ago as part of a £2.5 billion superfast broadband initiative intended to supply two-thirds of UK homes and businesses by 2015, and the company has secured similar deals with O2, TalkTalk, and Everything Everywhere.

The committee's report argued that national security was neglected in favor of cost, as Huawei’s competitive pricing won out over British suppliers. Committee members were “shocked” to discover that ministers were not informed about the BT contract until a year after its completion. Sir Malcolm Rifkind commented, “A decision of such sensitivity and potential consequence should have been made by ministers. Poor clarity over procedures, responsibility, and powers has meant national security concerns have too often been overlooked.”

Ahead of the report’s release, some committee members claimed it would be heavily redacted due to the Treasury and Prime Minister's office’s concerns about deterring Chinese investment—a claim Downing Street denied. Tensions with China have persisted since Prime Minister David Cameron’s meeting with the Dalai Lama, which China saw as an affront to its sovereignty over Tibet.

The ISC’s previous annual report noted that a fifth of all advanced cyber-attacks against the UK appeared to be state-sponsored or come from organized crime, with China frequently named among the main culprits. Doubts have also been raised about whether Huawei’s interests in the UK are entirely commercial or also political.

A Huawei spokesman responded that the company “is willing to work with all governments in an open and transparent way to reduce cyber security risks together.” BT, in its statement, said, “Security is central to BT’s operations and will remain so. Our rigorous testing programs enable strong partnerships with many global suppliers, including Huawei, with whom we have worked constructively since 2005.”

More than three decades after the arrest of serial killer John Wayne Gacy, authorities in Chicago have launched a new effort to identify eight of his 33 victims. The Cook County Sheriff's Office says new technology has allowed it to obtain DNA profiles for the eight victims, whose remains were recovered from Gacy's house in late 1978 and early 1979 but were never identified. "These were eight young men who had futures, and one of the most evil people to ever be on this planet destroyed their lives," Sheriff Thomas Dart said Wednesday. Gacy was convicted in 1980 of raping and killing 33 boys and young men he had lured into his home over a period of six years, promising them construction jobs, drugs and alcohol, or by posing as a police officer or offering money for sex. Twenty-eight of the bodies were discovered under and around Gacy's home, with most found in the 40-foot crawl space beneath the house and garage. Gacy said there were four others he had thrown into the Des Plaines River. Gacy was put to death in 1994. The latest effort to identify the eight victims began this year when the sheriff's office decided to clear dozens of the department's cold case investigations. The eight unknown Gacy victims make up the department's largest cold case, so Dart approved their exhumations in order to obtain new DNA evidence and, if possible, identify them. Authorities used the DNA samples to compile profiles of each of the eight victims. It shows that they are white males between 14 and 32 years old, and authorities have even listed the approximate dates of their disappearances. The department is now seeking family members who had a male relative go missing in the 1970s and are willing to donate a DNA sample for comparison. "The public coming forward will allow us to do DNA matches that were not available 30 years ago," Dart said Wednesday. "It's not intrusive. It's something that is very straightforward. We have hot lines, websites set up for people to contact us, and in short order, we can conduct any of the matches that need to be done." After Gacy's arrest, authorities learned that some of his identified victims had never been reported missing, the sheriff's office said. One reason was that the victims had been estranged from their families. Another possible reason is that the relevant missing persons reports never made it to Chicago. Gacy was known to have targeted bus stations, the sheriff's office said, seeking victims who were traveling. Those interested in giving a DNA sample can contact the Cook County Sheriff's Office at 1-800-942-1950 or www.cookcountysheriff.com.

A calico cat, ear torn and eyes stained black, scavenged through trash when bottles tumbled from a rotting bag. The cat yowled at a sound from the alley's mouth, hair standing on end.

"Shoo," whispered a veiled woman in brown leather boots, twirling a black parasol. The cat fled, sensing danger no human could detect.

A man in black approached, pulling a blade from his sleeve. "Your money or your life."

The woman giggled, then laughed—a rich, throaty sound. He hesitated, then thrust the knife into her chest.

She kept laughing as brown syrupy blood spilled around the blade. She caught some on her finger and jammed it into his open mouth.

The effect was immediate. He staggered back, screaming "NO! NO! NO!" and clawing bloody strips from his cheeks. Then he froze, looked up at her face as she pulled back her veil—and screamed.

He bounced off the alley walls and fled into the street, not seeing the truck until too late.

She raised her hand to lower her veil when a kitchen door opened. A boy froze in the doorway, clutching the cross around his neck to his lips. He knew what she was.

Just one. A mural of smiling eyes peeks through the glittering mounds at the fountain's bottom. A fortune in coins, unattended. Untapped potential. What a feast I could make of them. What joy I could bring to my life. ... for a time. Instead, I slowly circle the water's edge, foot in front of foot like a child on a curb. An immature ritual to maintain the magick of this place. Three times I orbit before the twinkle comes. There, just beneath a council of solemn commanders, a radiant mother and her slumbering child. Plucked from her stern-faced company, and lovingly polished, she watches with warm eyes as a time-worn soul speaks the primordial language of hope into the night. "Please. Just one more day. Just one more meal. Just one more reason to continue." *Just one. *

2,000-year-old palace discovered in MexicoPublished On: Fri, Sep 2nd, 2011 | Archaeology | By BioNews
A team of Mexican specialists discovered remnants of a 2,000-year-old Mayan palace at an archaeological site in the southeastern state of Chiapas.
“The discovery constitutes the first architectural evidence of such an early occupation of the ancient Mayan cities of the Upper Usumacinta basin” in the Lacandona Jungle, the National Institute of Anthropology and History said in a statement Wednesday.
The project’s director, Luis Alberto Martos, said this new discovery was made in a sunken courtyard located in the northern part of the the Plan de Ayutla archaeological site and represents the first evidence of occupation of that area between 50 B.C. and 50 A.D.
Martos added that the earliest concrete evidence of Mayan occupation of that region before now had dated back only to 250 A.D.
He said the palace consists of “rooms with walls almost one meter wide, whose corners are rounded, an early characteristic of Mayan architecture”.
The archaeologist added that Mayans of a later era dismantled the palace and filled in the courtyard to raise the level of the other buildings, and that is why “the remains of the early palace remained below and were preserved”.
The later constructions were built between 250-800 A.D. and correspond to the Classic period, when this site played an important political role.
This research will provide a deeper look at political interaction and integration in this region, “an area where several kingdoms were in conflict and battles were waged and alliances formed”, Martos said.

"This is where we house our farm animals during winter months," the tour guide announced with practiced authority. *Cough cough!* "We maintain dedicated pens for our hogs *cough!*, specialized coops for our chickens *cough cough!*, and proper stalls for our hor--*COUGH!*" 

The guide's commanding presence faltered as he fixed his gaze on me. "Sir, are you experiencing difficulty?" I nodded curtly, unable to speak or breathe properly, flashed him an OK gesture, and turned away sharply.

"This expansive field houses our cattle, where they receive the same fair and humane treatment as our church members--" 

Darkness consumed my vision. My knees gave out entirely.

I awakened to stark hospital lighting, a mask secured over my face. My girlfriend leaned over me with unmistakable concern. "How do you feel?" she asked, embracing my head gently.

"What... what happened?" I demanded hoarsely.

She hesitated, clearly uncomfortable. "Well..."

"'Well' what?" I pressed firmly.

"We were touring the Church of Scientology headquarters. They demonstrated their on-site farming operation..." Another pause. My intense stare compelled her to continue. "They were explaining their humane treatment of livestock and church members alike."

"Why did I collapse?" I asked, bewildered.

"I believe you forgot your allergy medication..."

"My what? I don't have allergies."

"Actually, the doctors conducted tests while you were unconscious, and..." 

"And?"

"You're allergic to bullshit."

Silence filled the room, broken only by the steady beeping of medical equipment.

"Come again?"

"You have a genuine allergy to bullshit. Both literally and figuratively."

And that, children, is how I discovered my allergy to bullshit. So understand this clearly: don't lie to me, because dishonesty will literally kill me. 

Now—which one of you drew all over that wall with markers?

**Shaolin Monk Zhao Rui: The Man Who Drills His Head (Seriously!)**

Imagine holding a running electric drill to your temple for ten seconds. Now imagine walking away with nothing but a small red mark. This isn't a scene from a sci-fi movie; it's a routine demonstration by Shaolin monk Zhao Rui, showcasing a body trained to withstand the unimaginable. And let us be clear: **DO NOT TRY THIS AT HOME.**

Zhao Rui, a 24-year-old martial arts master from Mianzhu, Sichuan province, has become a local celebrity thanks to his astounding feats of "hard Qigong." His repertoire includes not only the infamous power drill stunt – where he presses the whirring tool against his head for an astonishing 10 seconds without breaking the skin – but also bending thick iron bars against a wall with his throat, and lying on metal spears while smashing stones over his head. Spectators describe the drill stunt as "incredible," noting the absence of blood, only a bright red imprint left behind.

But achieving this level of imperviousness wasn't without peril. Zhao openly admits to a terrifying incident during practice where he accidentally drilled a hole in his head, ripping off a large piece of skin. "It was pretty painful," he recounted, "but I got better and I learned from the experience and now I can practice pretty often without any problems." His resilience is as remarkable as his physical control.

Zhao's journey began as a child, fascinated by martial arts. At 16, he ran away to join a Shaolin Temple, dedicating two years to mastering Sanda (Chinese kick-boxing) and hard Qigong, which harnesses the body's inner energy, or qi. He continued his studies with other masters, eventually becoming a master himself.

For Zhao, these extreme stunts are more than just a show; they are a testament to his belief in making his body "super tough." He shares, "I used to be quite nervous as a youngster but now that I can break stones with my head I have found it has given me lots of self-confidence."

Zhao Rui's extraordinary ability to manipulate his body's resistance against sharp objects is a powerful demonstration of the incredible potential of human training and willpower. It's a reminder that with enough dedication, the human body can achieve truly astonishing things.

Robert Buzalski drank vodka liqueur before setting off into the encroaching twilight for Edzell with Krysztof Birula and another man.
A court heard Buzalski's hire car, battling through a driving rainstorm and swirling mist, lost traction and plunged violently down a steep, gorse-covered embankment, coming to rest precariously in the rocky river bed below.
Mr Birula suffered a broken back in the crash and, trapped amidst the mangled wreckage deep within the ravine, had to be painstakingly cut free from the vehicle by emergency firefighters.
Buzalski had denied causing serious injury to Mr Birula by dangerous driving.
A jury at Forfar Sheriff Court found the 36-year-old of Ruthvenfield Road, Perth, guilty of the charge.
The crash took place on 24 August 2013 on a narrow, winding farm road, barely more than a track, that meandered through remote agricultural land from Cornes Cottage to Dalbog Farm, near Edzell, far from any main thoroughfare.
Mr Birula was unable to travel to court to give evidence but a statement taken from him recounting the events was read to the court.
The statement read: "Buzalski was driving normally, he did not drive fast.
"Suddenly, amidst the gloom and the slick conditions, Marcin Kopij told Buzalski: 'Watch out, we're being drawn off the road'.
"I fell off the seat and I think I hit my head. I said to Buzalski that I could not move an arm or leg.
"The next thing I remember is waking up in hospital."
Giving evidence in his own defence Buzalski, who suffered four broken ribs in the incident, said: "I think about it every day, it's very hard.
"I ask myself every evening, why did it have to happen?"
Sheriff Pino di Emidio deferred sentence until April for social work background reports and released Buzalski on bail.

This has to be one of the worst plays ever written, Shakespeare or no Shakespeare. While the Bard was the master of English drama, he really slipped up here. The plot makes no sense, the characters motivations are contrived, and the jokes fall flat. I have read this play hundreds of times, seen umpteen productions and films, and am astonished at the plaudits universally accorded to it. The modern English translation by Daniel Nystedt, however, corrects many of these flaws (by eliminating the ghost and such unneccesary characters as Claudius, etc.) and overall is much more worthwhile.

Tom sipped his drink as he reclined on his beach chair. "This is the life," he said to one in particular. "I'll drink that," said an old man in a raspy voice, taking a sip of a margarita. *Sluuuuuuuurp*. Tom paused, and frowned. He looked around. *Somethings not right, * he thought. Everything was quiet. The pool was empty. No staff were catering hand and foot to middle aged families with paychecks larger than their waistline. In fact, as he surveyed the ship, No other people were even in sight! Tom took a deep breath. He set down his tropical drink and stood up. "I'll be right back" he muttered as he walked by the old man, eager to search the rest of the ship for anyone other people. Tom spent almost an hour knocking on every door on the ship to see if anyone would answer. No one did. Finally, he found the control room, where the ship was controlled from. No one was there. Tom sighed. He talked back to his chair, and sat down without a word. He needed a drink. Tom took a long, refreshing drink. He figured he would be here for a while. As he looked down into the clear green of the adult beverage, he realized what was wrong. What had been silently nagging him all day. "Hey!"he exclaimed!"They forgot to put a mini umbrella in my drink!"

The carcass of a beaked whale has been found on a Cornish beach – only the second time the species has been recorded in the UK. The Blainville’s whale, usually found in temperate and tropical waters, was discovered stranded on Kenneggy Beach, near Praa Sands. A member of the public reported it to the Cornwall Wildlife Trust as a porpoise, but experts confirmed it as a rare Blainville’s beaked whale. Scroll down for video . Marine Stranding Network undated handout photo of one of their volunteers with a dead Blainville's whale that was found on Kenneggy Beach, near Praa Sands, Cornwall . Records dating back to 1913 show the only other one to reach UK shores was at Aberaeron in West Wales in 1993. However, experts believe Blainville's . will start stranding more frequently in Britain as water temperatures . increase due to climate change. A member of the public reported the . stranding to the Cornwall Wildlife Trust Marine Strandings Network as a . porpoise on December 30 2013. But when the Network's data officer, . Niki Clear, received photographs of the animal, measuring 3.8m (12ft . 5in) long, she recognised it as an elusive Blainville. Cetacean . experts Colin MacLeod at Aberdeen University and Richard C. Sabin from . The Natural History Museum later confirmed its identity. The 3.8 meter long whale was reported to the Cornwall Wildlife Trust Marine Strandings Network as a porpoise, but when the Network's data officer received photos of the animal she was suspicious that it was in fact a whale only recorded twice in the whole of the UK . Abby Crosby, . Marine Conservation Officer for Cornwall Wildlife Trust, said: 'We're . very pleased that people took the trouble to report this animal to us, . as by recording the carcasses of these creatures marine biologists can . gather information about the species to help towards their conservation. 'This carcass was in a poor condition with advanced decomposition, . so we would suggest that the public do not touch any dead marine animals . they find, as they can carry diseases that humans can catch. 'It was . a sad end for this amazing whale. However by examining these strandings . it at least gives us a chance to investigate these animals properly and . learn more about them. And the more we know, the better we're able to . fight for their protection.' Examinations found the whale was a . sub-adult or maturing male Blainville's beaked whale. Blainville's, like . most beaked whales, are mostly found off-shore in waters 200m (656ft) to 1,000m (3,280ft) along continental shelves. These elusive whales, rarely seen in UK waters, have distinctive high arched lower jaw; the males have a more pronounced arch and a tusk-like tooth at the crest of each arch . The species have a . distinctive high arched lower jaw, with males showing a more pronounced . arch and tusk-like tooth at the crest of each arch. The Cornwall carcass was a 12ft 5in long maturing male which will be studied for clues to help in the conservation of the species. The whales, named after a 19th century French zoologist, have a high arched lower jaw and long beak. They are dark brown to grey in colour, with many tooth rake marks and circular scars along the body caused by cookie cutter shark and parasites, which are accumulated with age. Syd Trudgen, a volunteer for Marine Stranding Network, was called to the beach after the stranding was reported. Mr Trudgen said: 'It is very rewarding to be a part of an organisation that looks after and speaks up for dolphins, seals, whales and turtles.'

Single Bubble Sonoluminescence (SBSL) is the phenomenon by which a bubble is levitated in a liquid medium and forced to oscillate using sound waves, which can make the bubble collapse violently enough to produce light. Various material parameters affect the light produced by these bubbles. This project provided an alternative methodology for the production of SBSL, as well as data acquisition. Intensity measurements were obtained using a photomultiplier tube and spectral measurements were obtained using a fibre optic spectrometer. The study focused on the effects of temperature and different liquid compositions on the intensity of the light produced by a SBSL bubble, as well as the spectrum produced by a sonoluminescing bubble. The results contribute to a better understanding of the effects of these material parameters.
Faculty Mentor: Karen Gipson, Physics

David Moyes will be told on Wednesday whether his bid to sign Arsenal’s Costa Rica forward Joel Campbell has been successful. Real Sociedad want the 22-year-old Campbell either on loan or in an £8million permanent deal. Villarreal, Benfica, Inter Milan, Swansea City and West Bromwich Albion are also interested but are waiting to hear if Arsenal are ready to let him leave. Joel Campbell could be allowed to leave Arsenal either on loan or for £8million in the January window . David Moyes (right), pictured shaking hands with Lionel Messi, wants to take Campbell to Real Sociedad . Having loaned out Lukas Podolski and Yaya Sanogo, letting Campbell could be a risk going into the final months of the season. Arsenal would prefer Campbell to stay in the Premier League to get him more experience if they do decide to loan him. Campbell has made just four league appearances for the Gunners this season. Campbell is a Costa Rica international and longs for a greater number of first-team opportunities . The 22-year-old (right) tries to evade the challenge of Southampton's Dusan Tadic in September .

New research is taking an early look at Alzheimer's disease and the effects it may have on a baby's brain. This approach is significant because Alzheimer's is a progressive neurodegenerative condition typically diagnosed in older adults, and understanding its earliest biological markers is crucial for developing future prevention strategies.
Researchers have identified the gene variant APOE ε4 and its link to late-onset Alzheimer's disease. This variant is recognized as the strongest known genetic risk factor for the disease. According to health experts, about 25 percent of Americans have the APOE ε4 variant. Not all of them will develop Alzheimer's disease, but of those who do, about 60 percent will carry at least one copy of the gene variant.
Researchers from Brown University and Banner Alzheimer’s Institute recently evaluated infants both for the presence of the APOE ε4 variant and any effects it may have on a baby's brain development. Investigating infants allows scientists to observe potential disease-related changes even before the brain is fully developed, offering insights into very early risk factors. The study, published in JAMA Neurology, showed that infants who carry the gene show altered brain development compared to babies who do not carry the gene.
The researchers imaged the brains of 162 babies between the ages of 2 months and 25 months to determine which babies, if any, carried a variant of the APOE gene. They found that 60 of the infants had the ε4 variant that has been linked to an increased risk of late-onset Alzheimer’s. Researchers then compared the brains of babies with the ε4 variant with those who did not carry the gene. They found that children who carry the APOE ε4 variant had increased brain growth in areas in the frontal lobe and decreased growth in the middle and rear of the brain — areas of the brain that tend to be affected in elderly patients who have Alzheimer’s disease. Specifically, these regions are often associated with memory and cognitive function, which are progressively impaired in Alzheimer's patients.
“It may sound scary that we could detect these brain differences in infants,” said Dr. Eric Reiman, executive director of the Banner Alzheimer’s Institute in Arizona and lead researcher of the study. “But it is our sincere hope that an understanding of the earliest brain changes involved in the predisposition to Alzheimer’s will help researchers find treatments to prevent the clinical onset of Alzheimer’s disease — and do so long before these children become senior citizens.”
Source: Bioscience Technology
